title: cerastium illyricum subsp. crinitum
intro: Cerastium illyricum subsp. crinitum, also known as the hairy snow-in-summer, is a subspecies of the Cerastium illyricum plant, which belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). The hairy snow-in-summer is a low-growing, herbaceous perennial that has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to soothe and calm digestive issues, reduce fever and pain, and promote wound healing. The herbal preparations of Cerastium illyricum subsp. crinitum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
